channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Parties jostle for Stafford byelection amid brief truce and political tensions
item.description: The upcoming Stafford byelection in Queensland has begun under unusual circumstances, with a temporary campaign truce observed during the funeral of late MP Jimmy Sullivan. While public campaigning pa...

item.title: Religious Groups Voice Opposition to Medically-Assisted Dying Proposal in South Africa
item.description: Several religious leaders in South Africa have strongly condemned Dignity SA's court application to decriminalise medically-assisted dying, arguing that it constitutes suicide, a practice prohibited a...

item.title: Analysis of the Liberal Democrats' Prospects Against Populist Rivals
item.description: The article examines the current political landscape in the United Kingdom, focusing on the performance and prospects of the Liberal Democrats compared with rising populist movements. It highlights th...

item.title: Project New Nigeria extends scholarship and digital support to Bayelsa students
item.description: Project New Nigeria don extend the support for Niger Delta students with scholarships and digital tools like iPads for those in Niger Delta University. Dis initiative, led by Samuel Kaley Nzidee, happ...

item.title: Federal Government reviews allowances and adds exit benefits for civil servants under pension scheme
item.description: The Federal Government don approve increase for key allowances wey civil servants dey receive, including Duty Tour Allowance (DTA), estacode, and book allowance. According to the Head of the Civil Ser...

item.title: Sarmad Khoosat Discusses ‘Lali’, Family, and Breaking Conventional Boundaries in Pakistani Cinema
item.description: Pakistani filmmaker Sarmad Khoosat discusses his latest feature film 'Lali', a Punjabi-language psychodrama exploring the complexities of marriage, family, and gender politics. Set in Sahiwal, Punjab,...

item.title: Yukon Explores Small Modular Reactors to Address Energy Needs
item.description: Officials from Yukon and Ontario have signed a partnership agreement to explore the use of small modular reactors (SMRs) as a solution to the territory's energy challenges. Yukon’s electricity grid is...

item.title: Ramaphosa calls on NBI to support South Africa’s economic transformation
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa addressed senior business and finance leaders during the 30th anniversary of the National Business Initiative (NBI), urging continued collaboration to achieve South Africa's ...

item.title: UN report ranks Pakistan among most affected countries by acute food insecurity amid climate and economic pressures
item.description: A UN-backed Global Report on Food Crises 2026 has placed Pakistan among the ten countries most affected by acute food insecurity, alongside nations like Afghanistan, Sudan and Yemen. The report highli...

item.title: Victor Gidado targets key role in Gaziantep’s crucial league matches
item.description: Talented Nigerian midfielder, Victor Ntino-Emo Gidado, don join Turkish Super Lig side Gaziantep FK for dem important league game against Eyupspor dis weekend. Gidado move come from Slovenian club NK ...

item.title: Ghana summons South African envoy over attacks on foreign nationals
item.description: Ghana has formally summoned South Africa's envoy following a series of xenophobic incidents affecting foreign nationals, including legal Ghanaian residents. Videos circulating online have highlighted ...

item.title: Awra Briguela Helps Jeepney Drivers by Giving Out Grocery Packs
item.description: In a heartwarming act of kindness, Awra Briguela, a well-loved Filipino celebrity, took the time to personally distribute grocery packs to jeepney drivers in need. On April 25, 2026, Briguela shared t...

item.title: Yahaya Bello Endorses Abejide’s Third Term Push for Yagba Constituency
item.description: Former Kogi State Governor, Yahaya Bello, has voiced his support for Leke Abejide’s third term bid in the upcoming 2027 elections. Speaking at a recent event organized by Abejide, who represents Yagba...

item.title: Legal challenge filed against Nigeria’s reintegration programme for former Boko Haram insurgents
item.description: One human rights lawyer, Maxwell Opara, don carry the Nigerian government and the army go court to challenge the ongoing reintegration of former Boko Haram fighters into society. The case, wey he file...

item.title: Top Nigerian Headlines For Saturday, April 25th, 2026: Xenophobic Attacks, Tinubu's New Policies & More
item.description: This roundup covers the most important headlines in Nigeria for Saturday, 25th April, 2026. Key stories include the Nigerian government's urgent warning to citizens in South Africa following an upsurg...

item.title: Tamil Nadu sees high voter turnout after Special Intensive Revision exercise
item.description: Tamil Nadu recorded an all-time high voter turnout of 85.1% in the recent assembly elections, according to provisional data from the Election Commission of India. This significant rise is largely attr...

item.title: Exceptional Fossils Preserved in Iron-Rich Sediments at McGraths Flat, Australia
item.description: Researchers have uncovered an extraordinary fossil site beneath the farmland of New South Wales, Australia, called McGraths Flat. Dating from 11 to 16 million years ago during the Miocene epoch, this ...

item.title: Pakistan Makes Significant Progress in Vaccinating Children Through Global Initiative
item.description: Pakistan has reaped substantial benefits from the international 'Big Catch-Up' (BCU) vaccination programme, a multi-year, multi-country effort led by Gavi, WHO, and Unicef to counter the decline in im...

item.title: Travellers seek stable homes amid growing push for settled living
item.description: The article examines how many travelling communities in the UK are increasingly seeking more stable, settled lives while trying to preserve their traditional way of life. Several families report a gro...

item.title: Australians defy government warnings to join Gaza humanitarian flotilla
item.description: Fifteen Australians are set to join the Global Sumud Flotilla, a civilian-led mission aiming to break Israel’s 19-year land and sea blockade of Gaza, despite strong warnings from the federal governmen...
